text: We describe a new open source, extensible and flexible software framework for Bayesian evolutionary analysis called BEAST 2. The BEAST 2 software framework has been designed from the ground up to address some of the structural issues that became apparent during the development of the highly successful BEAST 1 software framework. One of the major structural issues was a lack of post-deployment extensibility. The BEAST 2 software framework has a fully developed package management system, which enables third party developers to add new functionality to the software framework, in the form of a new package, which can then be installed directly into BEAST 2, from the Internet, without the need for a new software release. We describe a number of recently published additions to BEAST 2 in the form of new packages for modelling the process of evolution using a combination of a birth-death-sampling tree prior, phylodynamics and model averaging over different substitution models with the addition of site partitioning. We also describe how BEAST 2 facilitates checkpointing and provides a number of multi-processor and high performance computing extensions to the software framework. We also describe in greater detail than ever before the flexibility of the new software framework and how new functionality, which is made available as a new package, can be easily added to the user interface of BEAST 2 through a simple XML template-based mechanism. The software framework of BEAST 2 has been re-designed so that it and the user interface of BEAST 2, BEAUti 2, have greater cohesion to facilitate the software framework communicating with the user interface more effectively, for example the same XML files can be used for both the software framework and the user interface.
